113365.explanation: We are close to our residents and constantly in dialogue with them. This allows us to represent their interests well and weigh them carefully. Referendums often do not do justice to the complexity of important decisions.

113368.explanation: We have a positive attitude toward side activities at agricultural businesses, such as a shop or campsite. This can contribute to income and livability. However, it must fit the environment and requires a tailored approach, not automatic permission.

113370.explanation: We are not inherently against new distribution centers. If a location suits the site and does not have disproportionate effects on the surroundings, it may be possible. Careful consideration remains necessary.

113371.explanation: Eemsdelta already has a large asylum seekers center. We believe other municipalities should have their turn first. Distribution is important so that shelter is fairly divided.

113372.explanation: Housing seasonal workers on the company's own grounds is not always desirable. However, we believe seasonal workers should be housed differently and not in regular homes. We seek good alternative locations.

113376.explanation: A fireworks show can make New Year's Eve extra festive. We align with existing organizations that already organize many events. Together we make New Year's a great party for everyone.

113380.explanation: Housing construction on agricultural land is mainly a task of the province. The municipality can contribute ideas and provide tailored solutions but cannot independently enable this everywhere.

113382.explanation: The local broadcaster has recently received additional financial support. We consider this sufficient for now. We will continue to monitor the situation but currently see no need for extra money.

113383.explanation: In almost every village and neighborhood, new school buildings have been realized recently. Eemsdelta thus has very good and modern schools. Additional investments in all school buildings are not needed now.

113384.explanation: There are already arrangements for children from low-income families. Additionally, music education in schools is encouraged. We will continue to focus on that. Free music lessons for all children are not necessary in our view.

113386.explanation: Monuments are important for our village and cityscapes. We do not want to cut funding here, but also see no room for further budget expansion.

113388.explanation: Extra enforcement officers can contribute to safety. At the same time, safety is more than just officers on the street. It also involves good lighting and contact with residents, for example through neighborhood talks and coffee cart sessions.

113389.explanation: Where problems exist, camera surveillance can help. We are not against it but believe it should only be used if there are good and clear reasons.

113390.explanation: Victims of violence must be received quickly and safely. Good help and protection are essential. Whether this is organized locally or regionally must be carefully considered, but safety always comes first.

113392.explanation: Full priority for residents of Eemsdelta is not legally permitted. However, within the rules, we want to explore possibilities to better help people with ties to our municipality.

113394.explanation: This hardly occurs in Eemsdelta. But if legally possible and it helps reduce the housing shortage, we are not opposed. Of course, this must be carefully weighed.

113395.explanation: We prioritize infill development first, such as building on old school sites. But if adding a street is needed and fits well with the village, we are positively inclined.

113396.explanation: We support participation because being involved and having a purpose is good for people. At the same time, we believe this should not always be mandatory. There are situations where it doesn't work. That is why we mainly want to encourage and support.

113400.explanation: There is a need for more charging points, but this requires good consideration. Charging points should not stand empty unnecessarily. We assess what is needed per neighborhood and find trials with charging via lampposts interesting.

113401.explanation: A maximum speed of 30 km/h should not be implemented as a standard everywhere. Together with residents of villages and neighborhoods, we want to look at each situation to determine what is needed and desirable for traffic safety.

113402.explanation: We oppose segregation in care. However, there must always be room to be yourself and to consider someone's background. Eemsdelta is too small to have separate care institutions for every faith or conviction.

113404.explanation: We want to keep municipal tax burdens as low as possible, which has been successful in recent years. Further reduction of property tax (OZB) is now unrealistic given municipal tasks and reduced funds from The Hague.

113410.explanation: Currently, waste disposal at the recycling center is already partly free. We think this is a good balance. This keeps waste collection costs affordable for everyone.

113411.explanation: We are against large-scale solar parks on agricultural land. Small-scale, locally supported projects that are well integrated are possible. A complete ban does not fit with this.

113415.explanation: Attention to the Gronings dialect can be valuable, but we do not see this as a task or obligation for the municipality. Schools should be free to make their own choices.

113416.explanation: A new coffeeshop is not desirable; maintaining the current situation with one coffeeshop is preferred. This provides clarity, limits possible nuisance, and keeps supervision and enforcement manageable, thereby preserving livability and public order within the municipality.

113422.explanation: Local Interest Eemsdelta has no fixed position on this. The center of Appingedam is already car-reduced. Any further steps must be carefully examined, together with residents and entrepreneurs.

113423.explanation: The house of culture and administration is needed to keep theater and library affordable for the future and to make municipal accommodations more sustainable. Annual costs will be comparable to the current situation. We will ensure services remain nearby.

113424.explanation: The municipality helps where possible and already has a large earthquake department. Further expansion would come at the expense of other tasks or lead to higher taxes. Handling earthquake damage is ultimately not a municipal task.

113425.explanation: Measuring emissions around industry is mainly a task for the province and national government. We have urged for a comprehensive monitoring network and health research. If needed, we will see how the municipality can contribute, but not in advance.

113365.explanation: GreenLeft-Labour Party consider it important that residents should be able to express their opinions via referendums on major issues.

113368.explanation: By opening a shop or campsite, residents and farmers get to know each other better and farmers can supplement their income. This is also good for tourism.

113370.explanation: Eemsdelta is a large rural municipality but also has energy and industrial sites. If there is a need for a distribution center, the municipality must seriously investigate this.

113371.explanation: It is important to shelter people who are truly in need and have fled for their lives. Eemsdelta is already sheltering many refugees at present.

113372.explanation: It is important that seasonal workers find decent housing.

113376.explanation: The sale of fireworks has been banned since 2026. If groups of residents want to organize a fireworks show, the municipality should help by issuing a permit. Shooting with carbide is also a fun way to make noise.

113380.explanation: In consultation with residents, the municipality should decide together with farmers where building can take place.

113382.explanation: It is important to have a strong, independent local broadcaster.

113383.explanation: It is important that all children can attend school in a safe building with good ventilation.

113384.explanation: All children who want music lessons should be able to do so, even if their parents cannot afford it themselves.

113386.explanation: We must protect our villages and cities from demolition. Therefore, monuments are very important.

113388.explanation: If residents feel unsafe, the municipality must take action. For example, by deploying more public enforcement officers.

113389.explanation: If residents feel unsafe, the municipality must take action. For example, by installing cameras in certain locations.

113390.explanation: If it is necessary for someone to go to a women's shelter, the municipality must arrange that. This is best done in cooperation with other municipalities in our region.

113392.explanation: It is important that young people who want to stay living in our municipality have that opportunity.

113394.explanation: It is very difficult to find a home. As a temporary measure, holiday homes should be allowed to be used as permanent residences.

113395.explanation: If residents want to build new homes outside the village boundaries, this should be discussed with the residents and village to determine the best approach.

113396.explanation: It is important that everyone can participate in society, including people receiving social assistance benefits, but unfortunately not everyone can participate. Therefore, making participation mandatory is not good.

113400.explanation: It is important that there are enough places to charge electric cars. In addition, sufficient parking spaces must remain available for people with disabilities.

113401.explanation: We live in a large rural municipality with many villages. In consultation with the villages and residents, there should be consideration of where a maximum speed of 30 km/h and where 50 km/h is appropriate.

113402.explanation: There is a major staff shortage in healthcare. People should be able to choose a healthcare provider that suits them well, but that is not always possible.

113404.explanation: The property tax (OZB) must be balanced and comparable to other municipalities in our region.

113410.explanation: Residents who want to dispose of waste must be able to do so at the recycling center.

113411.explanation: Solar panels should be installed as much as possible on roofs (of large companies or farms).

113415.explanation: It is important for children to have a good understanding of the history of their own living environment. This also includes knowledge of the Gronings dialect.

113416.explanation: A coffeeshop may only be established if the residents agree with it.

113422.explanation: The center of Appingedam must be easily accessible for people from the surrounding area.

113423.explanation: A lot of money has already been invested in the plan. If we do not proceed with the house of culture and governance, we still need to invest in improving the old town halls, the library and the theater. It is cheaper to build one new building than several separate ones.

113424.explanation: Many people have problems with earthquake damage or strengthening their homes. The municipality must support residents as well as possible in this.

113425.explanation: All residents of Eemsdelta, even those living near industry, have the right to a healthy living environment!

113365.explanation: We are not in favor of a referendum. That is what elections every four years are for. The municipal council is the people's representative body and that comes with responsibility.

113368.explanation: We have no problem with farmers earning their living in broader areas. We do have the condition that it must remain small scale.

113370.explanation: When establishing new distribution centers, careful consideration must be given to their location. Appearance and traffic safety must be explicitly considered.

113371.explanation: The municipality of Eemsdelta already does a lot in shelter provision, including a regular asylum seekers center and sheltering Ukrainians. Other municipalities are first responsible. If there are local small-scale initiatives, we are certainly willing to explore the possibilities. Broad local support is a condition.

113372.explanation: Housing migrant workers on their own premises prevents nuisance in neighborhoods and villages. However, we do add the caveat that the safety and welfare of migrant workers must be guaranteed and maintained.

113376.explanation: Organizing fireworks shows is more suitable for local clubs and foundations.

113380.explanation: That said, we do want to keep control as a municipality over where housing expansion takes place and where it does not.

113382.explanation: Municipalities are legally responsible for supporting local broadcasters and that is good. Extra funding could undermine media independence.

113383.explanation: All schools in Eemsdelta have been strengthened and are thus structurally up to date.

113384.explanation: Just like sports, music education is very important for children. Subsidies should be available for parents who cannot afford it. Parents who can pay for it themselves do not need financial support.

113386.explanation: Cultural heritage is very important to the Christian Union; we must take good care of it.

113388.explanation: Recently, the municipality of Eemsdelta invested in the number of public enforcement officers (BOA's), so extra staff are not necessary in our view. However, the BOA's can still improve their visibility, especially in the villages.

113389.explanation: Wherever problems occur, camera surveillance should be explicitly deployed. Camera surveillance works preventively on the one hand and on the other hand offers more opportunities to identify perpetrators of nuisance. However, this is only used at nuisance locations.

113390.explanation: As a municipality, we must do everything possible to reduce violence against women. Regional agreements have been made about this, and in the municipality of Groningen there is a women's shelter. If the need arises, we are not opposed.

113392.explanation: We believe that people looking for rental housing in their own village or municipality should have priority. The housing shortage is certainly present in Eemsdelta; in our view, this shortage should not force people to have to live outside the municipality.

113394.explanation: The municipality of Eemsdelta does not have many holiday homes. Primarily, holiday homes are meant for holiday use, not residence. But in exceptional cases, there should be room for tailor-made solutions.

113395.explanation: The housing shortage is high, so you must dare to look beyond borders.

113396.explanation: This suggests there is always reluctance. Sometimes people are in a phase where they cannot work. Then the government is there as a fallback.

113400.explanation: At the moment, extra charging stations are being installed in many places. Let's first see if these meet the demand.

113401.explanation: As the Christian Union, we strongly support traffic safety. In our view, a 30 km/h zone certainly contributes to that.

113402.explanation: It is very important that when choosing a caregiver, you look for someone who aligns with your own beliefs.

113404.explanation: As the municipality of Eemsdelta, we rank among the lower regions in terms of local taxes. We must be cautious with increases, but reductions are currently not desirable.

113410.explanation: At the moment, bulky household waste can be disposed of for free three times. We think that is fine. For other waste streams, the polluter pays.

113411.explanation: As long as there are still enough roofs available, agricultural land must remain available for agriculture. If the energy transition requires more measures, initiatives by landowners should be possible under certain conditions.

113415.explanation: For cultural awareness among children, it would be good if they also become acquainted with Groningen culture and language.

113416.explanation: The use of substances does not contribute to the health of users, even if used occasionally. Additionally, substance use is often associated with criminal activities and causes a lot of nuisance. We would also prefer to see the existing coffee shop disappear.

113422.explanation: Given the historic character, narrow streets, and limited parking availability, it is not inviting to enter the Appingedam city center by car. A ban goes too far for us because it has adverse consequences for the entrepreneurs in the center of Appingedam.

113423.explanation: The current theater needs replacement and the current town halls require a substantial investment. New construction is ultimately more cost-effective. However, we must ensure that accessibility to municipal services is also guaranteed in the central villages and Appingedam.

113424.explanation: The central government is responsible for this. The municipality has no financial means to accommodate this.

113425.explanation: This is a provincial task and should therefore also be funded by the province/government. However, the municipality must commit itself to establishing such a monitoring network.

113365.explanation: Residents must be involved, but decisions belong to the elected municipal council.

113368.explanation: Farmers get space to operate with consideration for the environment and regulations.

113370.explanation: Distribution centers provide employment and economic growth.

113371.explanation: Shelter must be manageable and fit with support and capacity; Eemsdelta complies with the dispersal law.

113372.explanation: Employers are responsible for good and safe housing.

113376.explanation: This is not solely a municipal task; organizations/associations can also apply for a permit.

113380.explanation: This promotes flow and livability in rural areas.

113382.explanation: Independence of the media is paramount.

113383.explanation: Good school buildings are an investment in the future.

113384.explanation: Subsidy for music lessons must be linked to poverty policy, for those who need it.

113386.explanation: Monument care is valuable for identity and tourism, but expenses must be purposeful and, where possible, shared with owners and other parties.

113390.explanation: Yes to adequate, professional shelter for victims, but preferably through effective regional cooperation rather than an expensive own facility.

113394.explanation: Yes, but with a permit and suitability assessment.

113395.explanation: There must be room to build where necessary.

113396.explanation: Social assistance is a springboard, not a hammock.

113401.explanation: Depends on the situation; safety comes first.

113402.explanation: Freedom of choice for patients and care institutions is important.

113404.explanation: The VVD stands for low taxes, but only possible with a structurally balanced budget.

113410.explanation: Not everything free, but accessible. The VVD advocates the 'user pays' principle.

113411.explanation: No ban, but strict control. Food and livability come first, businesses can operate within limits, innovation gets a chance.

113415.explanation: Space for attention, but no obligation.

113416.explanation: Livability and safety come first. First repair national tolerance policy, then consider local expansion.

113422.explanation: The historic character demands respect, but the center must remain accessible.

113423.explanation: The building strengthens services and accessibility. Tourist attraction. Important social function for library and theater.

113424.explanation: For active municipal help and guidance residents must continue to be assisted. Payment comes at all costs through reinforcement tasks by the government.

113425.explanation: Measurement is needed for health and trust. The province must ensure reliable information provision in light of permit issuance. Conclusions should be reviewed by the Public Health Service (GGD).

113365.explanation: Referendums oversimplify complex decisions because people can only say yes or no. This means the difficulty of some plans is not taken into account. Referendums often cause more division among people, rather than a solution many people can support.

113368.explanation: The CDA believes that each situation should be assessed for what is possible. We are happy to think along with farmers about opportunities to earn additional income, such as a shop or campsite, as long as this fits with the environment and regulations.

113370.explanation: We believe that new distribution centers will not be established in our municipality quickly, so legislation is not necessary.

113371.explanation: At this moment, there are no new initiatives for the reception of asylum seekers.

113372.explanation: Employers are responsible for decent housing. They are also required to provide Dutch language lessons.

113376.explanation: We agree with this. The transition from the old to the new year should be celebrated together with residents. A municipal fireworks show, possibly in collaboration with local initiatives, can create a festive and safe moment.

113380.explanation: As far as the CDA is concerned, cities and villages remain the primary residential areas. We first look at whether we can add a street within our villages and cities before choosing this solution.

113382.explanation: The broadcaster receives a subsidy to professionalize; at this time, no other funds need to be called upon.

113383.explanation: The municipality has already made a significant investment in school buildings. Where necessary, we must continue to invest in these buildings, but much has already been done.

113384.explanation: This subsidy is already available. For example, all children and young people up to 18 years can use the Eemsdelta Instrument Fund.

113386.explanation: The CDA considers monument care important to preserve our cultural heritage. Budget cuts must be made carefully so that valuable monuments remain well maintained and preserved for future generations. Initiatives from society are also valuable.

113388.explanation: Recently, additional enforcement officers have been hired, so it is currently not necessary to expand the team. When this becomes necessary, the CDA considers safety to be a priority and this must be carefully considered.

113389.explanation: The CDA values safety. Surveillance cameras can help improve safety in Eemsdelta but must be used carefully and targeted, with respect for privacy and in consultation with residents. This certainly cannot be implemented everywhere indiscriminately.

113390.explanation: Shelter for people in need is already available in the region. The CDA finds it important that this shelter remains good and that people are helped safely and with support.

113392.explanation: The CDA wants to help everyone faster to an affordable and suitable home. The CDA wants at least 50% of the homes to go to local residents.

113394.explanation: Although the housing market requires creative solutions, we are in principle not in favor of permanent residence in vacation homes.

113395.explanation: The CDA believes that new construction must fit well with the village and the surroundings. Building outside village boundaries is allowed only if truly necessary. We opt for careful growth.

113396.explanation: The CDA believes that people receiving social assistance benefits can contribute to society where possible, for example through community work. This helps participation, development of talents, and having a positive role in the neighborhood.

113400.explanation: The CDA finds it important that the municipality thinks along about sustainable solutions. Charging points for electric cars are necessary to encourage electric driving and thus contribute to a cleaner living environment. Good investments have already been made here.

113401.explanation: The CDA values traffic safety. However, the current rules are sufficient at this moment.

113402.explanation: The CDA considers it important that everyone receives care that fits their faith, culture, or belief. Freedom of choice in care helps people feel safe and comfortable.

113404.explanation: The CDA understands that lower taxes are appreciated by residents. But we also believe the municipality needs sufficient income to provide good services. Therefore, we always carefully consider how to keep taxes fair and affordable.

113410.explanation: The CDA considers the current situation adequate. Some waste can be disposed of free of charge; residents pay a contribution for demolition waste, bulky waste, and soil.

113411.explanation: The CDA believes agricultural land should primarily remain for food production. Solar panels on agricultural land are allowed only if really necessary and no better alternatives exist. We always seek a good balance between energy, nature, and agriculture.

113415.explanation: The provincial identity is considered by the CDA to be valuable to pass on to future generations.

113416.explanation: The CDA is cautious about opening new coffee shops. Safety, health, and livability in the neighborhood are important.

113422.explanation: Although the character of Appingedam requires careful consideration regarding traffic, making it completely car-free is, according to the CDA, not feasible and impractical.

113423.explanation: The CDA supports merging municipal offices in combination with our theater and library. However, we are critical and attentive to the costs involved.

113424.explanation: Residents deserve all help regarding earthquake damage. The municipality is not the appropriate body for this issue. However, the municipality should provide good assistance and support and communicate carefully.

113425.explanation: The municipality must take care of the health of our residents. If good measurements around our industry are necessary for this, the CDA is open to thinking along about it.

113365.explanation: Better to have a citizens' council and residents' meetings, possibly preceded by a survey. A referendum offers little nuance; emotion and framing play a role and there is no room for dialogue.

113368.explanation: Allowing everything can lead to more noise nuisance, litter, traffic nuisance, safety issues, and nitrogen problems.

113370.explanation: They can have economic benefits but place too much pressure on livability and the environment due to truck traffic, air pollution, noise nuisance, and space consumption. Often there is no employment for local residents either.

113371.explanation: We already have an asylum seekers center.

113372.explanation: Take care of proper housing yourself. Also, provide aftercare for a period after they no longer work there.

113376.explanation: A compensation as an alternative to the fireworks ban. It can also be a light show without bangs. This is our preference because of the animal suffering caused by fireworks.

113380.explanation: Take into account zoning plans and the Environment and Planning Act; do not build just anywhere but exceptions should be possible if well justified.

113382.explanation: The local broadcaster consists of volunteers but is not a luxury. A local broadcaster is an indispensable basic provision for a well-functioning local democracy. The municipality should therefore support this, including in a well-accessible studio.

113383.explanation: Schools should be equipped with a good climate control system such as air conditioning.

113384.explanation: Culture is important for everyone and promotes health. A music school like the one that once existed (IVAK) should be reconsidered.

113386.explanation: Cultural education is only successful with attention to cultural history and heritage.

113388.explanation: At least better enforcement by police and community officers.

113389.explanation: Only in cases of serious problems and then temporarily.

113390.explanation: That would be a good addition.

113392.explanation: Preventing disruption, so avoiding unfair displacement. Priority contributes to a liveable, engaged community.

113394.explanation: At least as a temporary option due to the housing shortage.

113395.explanation: It has pros and cons. Proper research into usefulness and necessity must be conducted. It affects the landscape, climate, and nature but also offers opportunities to build new homes.

113396.explanation: To get people active in society again, it is important that they remain involved in the community.

113401.explanation: That would be a very good measure, but it must also be enforced.

113402.explanation: That would be an empty promise because it cannot be realized due to the shortage of caregivers.

113404.explanation: Not lower, but also no increase because in 2025 we have already gone quite high (9.6%). That is well above the national average of 5%.

113410.explanation: Yes, that is very important to properly manage all waste streams and to prevent nuisance and dumping.

113411.explanation: No large-scale solar parks on agricultural land.

113415.explanation: Important because of culture and the quality of language lessons. Bilingual education is very good for children's development.

113416.explanation: There is one. Expansion leads to more nuisance, more police deployment. It also affects youth: the chance they come into contact with drugs sooner and more often increases, which is undesirable.

113422.explanation: A low-car or car-free center driven by sustainability, safety, and livability, with a practical approach to minimize impact on the historic center.

113423.explanation: Arguments in favor: 1 - synergy and efficiency. 2 - meeting place provides connection and great opportunities.

113424.explanation: Because residents find the current system slow, unclear, and insufficient, with many financial shortages and uncertainties. Additional fixed compensation and subsidies facilitate and accelerate.

113425.explanation: Absolutely necessary to limit health risks and curb nuisance from odor and noise.

113365.explanation: Volt believes that residents are co-owners of decisions affecting their living environment. A referendum can strengthen that involvement if used carefully and if it is clear what happens with the outcome. This makes democracy active and meaningful.

113368.explanation: Volt wants to give farmers space to operate and make their businesses future-proof. Side activities such as a shop or campsite can help. This must fit the environment, livability, and spatial agreements. Custom solutions come first, no automatic right.

113370.explanation: Volt wants to protect the Groningen landscape and the livability of villages. Distribution centers can cause extra traffic, emissions, and pressure on the landscape but can also provide employment. Each location must be assessed to determine whether the social benefits outweigh the impact.

113371.explanation: Volt stands for humane and well-organized refugee reception. An asylum seeker center and reception have already been realized in Eemsdelta. The question of whether expansion is necessary should be assessed based on carrying capacity, available facilities, and the quality of reception.

113372.explanation: Volt believes that migrant workers must have good and safe housing. Employers have responsibility, but mandatory on-site housing is not always appropriate. The municipality must set clear standards and maintain good supervision.

113376.explanation: Volt is proud of the strong community in Eemsdelta, where old and new are already celebrated together through local traditions and initiatives. We want to strengthen that strength. A fireworks show organized by an association can fit in, with municipal support if needed.

113380.explanation: Volt sees that Eemsdelta urgently needs more housing. Initiatives by farmers to build homes can contribute to this. This must be done carefully, fitting the landscape and living environment, and as part of a thoughtful spatial development.

113382.explanation: Volt views local media as indispensable for democracy and community spirit. A strong local broadcaster provides independent information, involved residents, and insight into what happens in villages and neighborhoods. Support helps maintain that role.

113383.explanation: Volt believes every child has the right to a safe, healthy, and future-proof learning environment. Although many schools have already been reinforced, continued investment is needed to guarantee quality, sustainability, and well-being for current and future pupils.

113384.explanation: Volt believes every child should have the opportunity to discover talents and develop culturally. Music education contributes to creativity, self-confidence, and equal opportunities. Municipal support ensures music lessons remain accessible to all children.

113386.explanation: Volt wants to preserve Eemsdelta's heritage and pass it to future generations. Monuments tell the story of our villages and region. Cuts to monument care undermine that identity and harm livability, culture, and tourism.

113388.explanation: Volt wants a safe municipality but does not see enforcement as an end in itself. The deployment of community officers must be targeted and proportional, aligned with needs in villages and neighborhoods. Prevention, social cohesion, and good public spaces remain just as important.

113389.explanation: Volt values both safety and privacy. Camera surveillance can be a supplement at specific risk locations but is not a standard solution. It must demonstrably contribute to safety, be used proportionally, and carefully respect residents' privacy.

113390.explanation: Volt believes residents experiencing domestic violence must receive quick and safe help. Good and accessible shelter is important. A women's shelter can contribute to protection and recovery, in connection with existing care and support.

113392.explanation: Volt wants people who live or grew up in Eemsdelta to also be able to build a future here. With housing scarcity, local ties may count in housing allocation to keep villages livable and maintain social cohesion.

113394.explanation: Volt favors flexible housing solutions to tackle the housing shortage. Permanently living in holiday homes can help, for example for starters. This must be done carefully to preserve recreation and area quality.

113395.explanation: Volt wants careful use of space and landscape. By primarily building within existing village boundaries, the open Groningen landscape is preserved and villages are strengthened from within. Expansion outside the core can only be considered when truly necessary.

113396.explanation: Volt is based on trust and humanity. Participation is important but should not be a mandatory condition for basic security. The municipality must support people based on their personal situation, focusing on development, reintegration, and prospects for suitable work.

113400.explanation: Volt wants to make sustainable mobility accessible to everyone. Sufficient charging points are necessary to enable electric driving, also for residents without a private driveway. Expanding the number of charging spots accelerates the energy transition fairly.

113401.explanation: Volt wants safe and liveable villages and neighborhoods. Lower speeds can improve traffic safety and livability, especially in residential streets and small centers. At the same time, Eemsdelta has main roads where higher speeds are logical. Therefore, careful assessment per street is needed.

113402.explanation: Volt believes care must be person-centered. The ability to choose a caregiver that matches someone's background can contribute to trust and quality of care. This must happen within feasible and affordable frameworks.

113404.explanation: Volt believes that Eemsdelta must remain livable, with facilities that residents use daily. The property tax (ozb) is a stable source of income and ensures a fair distribution: those who own more contribute more. This helps maintain the quality of our municipality.

113410.explanation: Volt wants to encourage waste separation while also handling costs fairly. Making the recycling center completely free leads to higher waste charges for everyone. This shifts costs without actually solving the waste problem.

113411.explanation: Volt aims to accelerate the energy transition, but carefully. Solar panels on agricultural land are not the first choice. Roofs of homes and businesses take priority to prevent landscape pollution. Only where this is not possible can agricultural land be considered, in consultation with farmers and the community.

113415.explanation: Volt wants to cherish the Groningen regional language as a living part of our identity. By giving attention to Gronings in primary schools, we provide children with knowledge of their own culture and strengthen language awareness and pride.

113416.explanation: Volt supports a realistic and regulated drug policy. With only one coffeeshop, street dealing continues and young people unnecessarily come into contact with drugs. More coffeeshops with clear rules and supervision increase safety, limit crime, and remove trade from the underground.

113422.explanation: Volt desires a center that is pleasant and safe for residents and visitors. Less car traffic can contribute to this. However, a fully car-free center would hinder accessibility and traffic flow in Appingedam. Therefore, a targeted approach is needed, not a complete closure.

113423.explanation: Volt recognizes the importance of a well-functioning municipal organization. Appropriate housing can contribute to this. At the same time, we are critical of costs and execution. The house of culture and government must be a place of and for the community, with transparency and participation.

113424.explanation: Volt wants residents with earthquake damage to not be left alone. The municipality must actively support them during damage handling and reinforcement. The financial responsibility primarily lies with the national government and involved agencies, but the municipality must stand alongside the residents.

113425.explanation: Volt wants a healthy living environment and transparency for residents. Measuring emissions around industry provides insight, increases trust, and enables targeted enforcement. The municipality must invest in this and hold companies accountable.

113365.explanation: Decisions about our villages and neighborhoods should be made together with residents. Too often, plans are pushed through, from wind turbines to closing facilities. A binding corrective referendum is a key point of the SP.

113368.explanation: Farmers should be able to earn extra income through recreation and local sales, such as camping at the farm, farm shops, and farmers' markets. This way, farms remain viable and residents and tourists benefit from our beautiful countryside.

113370.explanation: We choose qualitative economic growth: growth that leads to better wages, permanent jobs, local production, and production that respects people, the environment, and the living environment. Distribution centers do not provide that qualitative growth.

113371.explanation: The SP supports the distribution law so that every municipality contributes to sheltering people fleeing war and violence. Neighborhoods already under pressure should not be additionally burdened. It is therefore wiser to locate shelters in affluent neighborhoods.

113372.explanation: When your employer is also responsible for your housing, you become highly dependent on your boss. If you complain or don’t work hard enough, you risk not just your job but also your roof. This situation makes it easier for employers to exploit labor migrants.

113376.explanation: As an alternative, it is good to organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.

113380.explanation: Houses are meant to live in, not to make money from. Land must be used to build affordable housing, not to make a profit. Furthermore, agricultural land is needed for food production.

113382.explanation: Through Omroep Eemsdelta we not only hear what the major national broadcasters say but also the stories of people, entrepreneurs, and politicians from our villages. This gives us more involvement, ensures better oversight of local politics, and is essential for a living local democracy.

113383.explanation: Besides buildings needing to be safe, healthy, and sustainable, investments must also be made not only in the physical structures but also in collaboration with WIJ teams, in traffic safety around schools, and in green schoolyards and playgrounds that contribute to a healthy living environment.

113384.explanation: Art and culture are part of every child's development. That is why we invest in cultural education at school, with specialist teachers and cooperation with cultural institutions, so every child can develop their creative talents regardless of their parents' financial situation.

113386.explanation: Our characteristic buildings are part of Eemsdelta’s identity: the hanging kitchens, the old churches on the ancient mounds, and the Amsterdam School style buildings from the last century. Without these roots, there is no future. Our heritage is worth protecting.

113388.explanation: Police officers and community enforcement officers (BOAs) must be fairly distributed across all neighborhoods, so every village and district is safe. Police and BOAs must also receive sufficient personnel and resources to do their jobs well.

113389.explanation: Privacy is a fundamental right and cameras should only be used if the issue cannot be solved in other ways, for example with extra BOAs, officers, or youth work. It is better to address the causes of crime or nuisance.

113390.explanation: No woman should feel unsafe. Signs of imminent violence must be recognized faster and timely intervention must occur. There should also be an approach that looks at the root causes.

113392.explanation: Those who need housing most urgently must be prioritized, for example homeless people with children or women threatened by their partners. These are often people from our own municipality. Additionally, more homes must be built so that prioritization becomes unnecessary.

113394.explanation: It is better to create more available housing through new construction and converting vacant buildings. The municipality should also stop issuing fines for cohabitation so that homes are used optimally.

113395.explanation: It should be the residents of the village who decide on this.

113396.explanation: Coercive measures do not help people, and we will stop systematically distrusting those on welfare. People with disabilities have the right to guidance toward suitable work through a work development company (formerly social workplace) with a full wage and a good collective labor agreement.

113400.explanation: Anyone driving electric vehicles must be able to charge fairly and affordably anytime and anywhere. Charging stations should be managed by the municipality. Not a profit model for companies, but a reliable service for everyone. The neighborhood should be involved in decisions about their locations.

113401.explanation: Traffic safety must be in order everywhere, starting with schools and shopping centers. It must always be safe to walk or cycle on any street.

113402.explanation: Dignified care must come first, with attention to individual needs. Origin, religion, or beliefs should never be barriers to giving or receiving care. Good care is equally accessible for everyone.

113404.explanation: To maintain social facilities, the property tax (OZB) may be increased. Those with the greatest means should bear the heaviest burdens.

113410.explanation: Waste collection and processing is a social task; no one should profit from it. Making this service free promotes a clean living environment. We also support local repair workshops to make repairing affordable and accessible for everyone.

113411.explanation: The SP wants a municipal energy company that installs solar panels on all suitable roofs to lower energy bills. By prohibiting private project developers, we prevent them from starting solar parks solely for profit without residents benefiting.

113415.explanation: The cultural wealth of the Netherlands does not stop at museum doors. It lives in the languages of our neighborhoods and communities, such as Gronings. We choose to actively support this in education. Culture belongs to all of us and must not be cut or pushed aside.

113416.explanation: We do not encourage drug use, but we do not criminalize users either. The SP supports regulating and legalizing soft drugs. This allows better control of safety and quality. Everyone who wants to quit has the right to support.

113422.explanation: Walking and cycling are healthy and environmentally friendly. But it must be safe to walk and cycle. Traffic safety must be in order everywhere, starting with schools and shopping centers. The center of Appingedam is suitable to become car-free if there is sufficient parking space around it.

113423.explanation: The SP finds it important that everyone can practice sports, visit the library, cinema or theater, or take music lessons regardless of income. Associations and volunteers deserve structural support and appreciation. It is better to support associations and people than an expensive building.

113424.explanation: There is a fund at the municipality to solve inequality. That fund must be used for this purpose. Additionally, the people of batch1588 have been waiting for years for reimbursement of costs that the municipality still must repay.

113425.explanation: The economy must not come at the expense of health. With the new zoning plan, more factories will appear that emit highly hazardous substances. That is why the SP, together with residents, has been advocating for structural and transparent monitoring of air quality and noise for years.

statements.title: The municipality should invest more money in assistance for people whose homes have been damaged by earthquakes.
The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety should be deployed in Eemsdelta.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: Residents of Eemsdelta should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Eemsdelta moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Eemsdelta.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in a system that measures harmful emissions around the industrial areas near Eemshaven and Delfzijl.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The center of Appingedam should be made car-free.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to give attention to the Gronings dialect.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
A domestic violence shelter should be established in Eemsdelta.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should block the opening of a new coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should cancel the plan for a new cultural and municipal center.
